hawkish_phrases: job gains have been solid, on average, in recent months
strong labor markets and rising incomes continued to support the outlook for consumer spending
the Committee decided to maintain the target range for the federal funds rate at 2-1/4 to 2-1/2 percent
dovish_phrases: uncertainties and downside risks surrounding the economic outlook had increased significantly
inflation continued to run below the Committee's 2 percent objective
Market-based measures of inflation compensation have declined
many judged that additional policy accommodation would be warranted in the near term
the Committee will closely monitor the implications of incoming information for the economic outlook and will act as appropriate to sustain the expansion
summary: The Committee voted 9-1 to hold rates at 2.25-2.50% with James Bullard dissenting in favor of a 25bp cut, removing prior 'patient' language and signaling that rates may need to be lowered soon given increased uncertainties, muted inflation pressures, and downside risks to the outlook.
